nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving streams, marshes, swamps, and rice paddies of the South Asian subcontinent, typically over sandy or muddy substrates where it tends to form dense colonies along the banks.

decorPreferences: open background or midground space for tall narrow leaves

summary: A tall, grass-like crypt from India and Sri Lanka with narrow leaves that twist into a slight spiral. Generally considered one of the easier Cryptocoryne species and less prone to crypt melt.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne spiralis is commonly described as one of the more forgiving Cryptocoryne species and is well suited to beginners looking to try a crypt for the first time. Its tall, narrow, ribbon-like leaves develop a slight spiral twist and a crinkled edge, making it a natural background or tall-midground plant in medium-to-large aquariums. It tends to do best in low-to-medium light over a nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs; CO2 is not required, but supplementation has been reported to encourage faster growth and stronger coloration. Like other Cryptocoryne, it can melt after relocation or sudden water-parameter shifts, but most sources report that it is noticeably more melt-resistant than wendtii/parva and tends to recover from the rhizome within a couple of weeks if left undisturbed. Source spread on adult height was wide (Tropica reports 20-50+ cm for the 'Red' cultivar, Fish Laboratory cites up to ~60 cm, Light Fish reports up to ~60 cm, Aqualand reports 20-50 cm) — taken here as roughly 25-50 cm. Temperature consensus is ~22-28°C; Fish Laboratory uniquely reports a cooler 18-24°C window, which was averaged in but not used as the upper bound. pH consensus is 6.0-7.5, with some sources tolerating up to 8.0. The 'Red' and 'Tiger' cultivars are colored selections of the same species sold alongside the standard green form.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica's catalog only lists the 'Red' cultivar pages; the search root redirected here as the authoritative Tropica spiralis entry. Used to cross-check leaf morphology (1-1.5 cm wide, softly waved), adult height (20-30+ cm, over 50 cm in mature tanks), medium light, CO2 6-14 mg/L, and Indian origin.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Designated secondary. Confirmed long slender crinkled leaves, background placement, and easy-to-grow care level. Did not provide numeric water parameters.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Third source used to cross-check pH (6.0-8.0), adult size (25-61 cm), Indian origin, low-to-moderate light, optional CO2, and crypt-melt-on-disturbance behavior. Reports a cooler 18-24°C window that disagrees with the other sources' 22-28°C consensus.

additional.name: Light Fish
additional.notes: Fourth source used to cross-check temperature (~23-26°C), pH (6.0-8.0), gH (1-20), kH (1-18), Western Indian rice-paddy origin, and beginner-friendly care label. Hardness range is broader than other sources; averaged to 2-12 dGH/dKH.

additional.name: Aqualand Pets Plus
additional.notes: Fifth source used to confirm broader native range (India, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, Myanmar), 22-28°C temperature, pH 6.0-7.5, dKH 2-12, slow-to-moderate growth, background placement, and propagation via runners and rhizome division.
